content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Retrieval-Augmented Self-Verification Prompting for Factual Consistency in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently generate factual errors or hallucinations due to insufficient grounding in external knowledge. Existing prompting techniques often lack mechanisms for explicit fact-checking within the generation process.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are adopted in critical domains, it is essential to ensure factual accuracy and reduce hallucinations. Current static retrieval-augmented prompting methods provide limited improvement, since they integrate evidence only once prior to generation. Dynamic, in-context self-verification could offer stronger factuality guarantees by continuously referencing external sources throughout the generation process.",
  "method": "We propose a two-stage prompting method: (1) The LLM generates an initial answer using a standard query, augmented with retrieved documents. (2) A specialized 'fact-verifier' prompt then instructs the LLM to identify potentially factual claims in the draft answer, retrieve relevant evidence for each, and explicitly revise or annotate the answer in light of retrieved content. This process can be iteratively applied, creating a feedback loop for self-correction. The method is model-agnostic and uses in-context demonstrations to guide verification behavior.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate our method on fact-checking QA datasets (e.g., FEVER, TruthfulQA, HotpotQA). Baselines include standard LLM prompting, single-pass retrieval-augmented generation, and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include factual consistency (human and automated), hallucination rate, and answer quality. Ablation studies will test the effect of each stage and iteration."
}
